issues.title: Missing filter for transaction status
issues.problem: The query should only include transactions with status 'COMPLETED' for calculating lifetime value.

issues.title: Incorrect aggregation
issues.line: SUM(t.amount) AS lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS total_orders
issues.problem: The query should only sum the amount for completed transactions, but it is not filtering for 'COMPLETED' status in the aggregation.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS lifetime_value

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, making it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.line: SUM(t.amount) AS lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS total_orders
issues.problem: The aliases 'lifetime_value' and 'total_orders' are not consistently named. Consider using 'total_lifetime_value' and 'total_transactions'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS total_transactions

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) AS total_transactions FROM dim_customer c LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY total_lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query has correctness issues with aggregation and missing status filter. Readability can be improved with comments and consistent naming.

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly by dividing the failed_count by total_count for each row, instead of calculating it once per transaction_date.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN to avoid correlated subquery.

issues.title: Lack of comments on complex logic
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which can make it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql: WITH failed_transactions AS (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS failed_count FROM fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED' GROUP BY transaction_date), total_transactions AS (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS total_count FROM fact_transactions GROUP BY transaction_date) SELECT t.transaction_date, ft.failed_count, tt.total_count, (ft.failed_count::DECIMAL / tt.total_count) * 100 AS failure_rate FROM total_transactions tt LEFT JOIN failed_transactions ft ON tt.transaction_date = ft.transaction_date ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had correctness issues with the failure rate calculation, performance issues with the correlated subquery, and readability issues due to lack of comments.

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: Revenue should only include transactions with status 'COMPLETED'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS revenue_usd

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose and logic of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT c.customer_name,
c.email,
m.merchant_name,
SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS revenue_usd,
COUNT(*) AS transaction_count
FROM fact_transactions t
JOIN dim_customer c
  ON t.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN dim_merchant m
  ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.transaction_date >= '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY c.customer_name,
c.email,
m.merchant_name
ORDER BY revenue_usd DESC;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and lacked readability due to missing comments.

issues.title: Missing filter for transaction status
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status, which means it will include all transactions regardless of their status.

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can make the query less readable and harder to maintain.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic, which can make it harder for others to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose and logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(t.amount) AS total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) AS txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
  AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
  AND t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query has a critical correctness issue by not filtering completed transactions and uses an implicit join syntax. It also lacks comments for readability.
